channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Walz says he's 'in touch with Delta' after crash landing at Toronto airport
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D) said Monday he was “in touch with Delta” following the crash landing of a flight at Toronto Pearson International Airport. The flight originated at Minneapolis-St. Paul International Airport (MSP) in Walz's state.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Middle East latest: Israelis and Palestinians mark 500 days of war in Gaza with more uncertainty
item.description: Israelis have marked 500 days of war in Gaza with protests demanding further progress on a ceasefire so all remaining hostages can be returned. People demonstrated in Tel Aviv and Jerusalem on Monday as less than two weeks remain in the ceasefire's current phase.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Many weary Ukrainians long for an end to the war but now fear it will come on unfavorable terms
item.description: After nearly three years of living under constant threat of Russian airstrikes while their troops fight a grinding campaign against Russia’s invasion, many Ukrainians long for an end to the war.
item.dc:creator: Justin Spike, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Egypt is developing a plan to rebuild Gaza, countering Trump's call to depopulate the territory
item.description: Egypt is developing a plan to rebuild Gaza without forcing Palestinians out of the strip in a counter to President Donald Trump’s proposal to depopulate the territory so the U.S. can take it over.
item.dc:creator: Samy Magdy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Rwanda-backed rebels tighten their grip on a major city in eastern Congo
item.description: Rwanda-backed rebels have tightened their grip on Bukavu a day after seizing the second major city in eastern Congo.
item.dc:creator: Pascal Cinamula And Justin Kabumba, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Rwanda-backed M23 rebels occupy a 2nd major city in Congo's mineral-rich east
item.description: BUKAVU, Congo (AP) — Rwanda-backed rebels have occupied a second major city in mineral-rich eastern Congo, the government said Sunday, as M23 rebels confirmed they were in the city to restore order after it was abandoned by Congolese forces.
item.dc:creator: CHINEDU ASADU, PASCAL CINAMULA and JANVIER BARHAHIGA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli military experts weigh in on Trump's ‘all hell’ threat to Hamas and what it could look like
item.description: Former Israeli military officials discuss how President Donald Trump's threats against Hamas could play out in Gaza, and what strategies the IDF could adopt if they go back into the enclave.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top Russian and American officials will hold talks on ending the Ukraine war without Kyiv
item.description: The Kremlin says senior American and Russian officials will hold talks on improving their ties and negotiating an end to the war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: China says US has 'gravely backpedaled' on Taiwan
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — China's Foreign Ministry took issue Monday with a revised U.S. government fact sheet that removed a line on American opposition to independence for Taiwan.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 500 days of the Israel-Hamas war, by the numbers
item.description: Monday is the 500th day of the war triggered by Hamas' Oct. 7, 2023 attack into southern Israel. A tenuous ceasefire in the Gaza Strip has held for nearly a month.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Australia plans to deport 3 violent criminals to Nauru
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Australia will soon deport three violent criminals including a convicted murderer to Nauru even though they are not citizens of the tiny Pacific atoll, after an Australian court ruled that dangerous immigrants can no longer be imprisoned indefinitely.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Zelenskyy travels to UAE as momentum grows for talks to end Russia's war in Ukraine
item.description: Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskyy traveled to the United Arab Emirates as momentum grows for potential peace talks ending Russia's war.
item.dc:creator: Jon Gambrell, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Woman killed as Israeli forces fire on returnees to southern Lebanon ahead of ceasefire deadline
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — A woman was killed and several other people wounded Sunday when Israeli forces opened fire on a group of residents attempting to return to the village of Houla in southern Lebanon, the Lebanese state-run news agency reported.
item.dc:creator: ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says Putin 'wants to stop fighting' in Ukraine
item.description: President Trump said Russian President Vladimir Putin “wants to stop fighting” and shot down a suggestion that Putin’s ambitions could extend beyond Ukraine’s borders. “I think he wants to stop fighting. I see that. We spoke long and hard.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Suspect in fatal stabbing in Austria had an 'Islamic terror motive,' officials say
item.description: VIENNA (AP) — Austrian authorities said Sunday the stabbing of six people that left a 14-year-old boy dead was carried out by a man with possible connections to the Islamic State group who appeared to have acted alone.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANIE LIECHTENSTEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Austria stabbing attack suspect is Syrian migrant who pledged allegiance to Islamic State, officials say
item.description: Austrian officials said a Syrian migrant carried out an IS-motivated knife attack a day after Vice President JD Vance derided EU leaders on mass migration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: President Trump urged to confront Iranian regime over repression of Christians
item.description: Iran’s repression of Christians continues to grow, according to advocacy groups and analysts. Earlier this month the regime re-arrested two men who had previously been jailed due to their faith.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukraine says US presented doc to access minerals, offered almost nothing in return
item.description: “I didn’t let the ministers sign a relevant agreement because in my view it is not ready to protect us, our interest,” Zelenskyy told The Associated Press on Saturday in Munich.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White South Africans gather in support of Trump and his claims that they are victims of racism
item.description: PRETORIA, South Africa (AP) — Some white South Africans showed support for President Donald Trump on Saturday and gathered at the U.S. Embassy in Pretoria to claim they are victims of racism by their own government.
item.dc:creator: ALFONSO NQUNJANA and GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Emmanuel Macron calls 'emergency meeting' for European leaders to discuss Trump: report
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron has allegedly planned an "emergency meeting" to discuss President Donald Trump on Monday, according to a Polish official.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rwanda-backed rebels advance and Congolese forces retreat from eastern Congo's second largest city
item.description: GOMA, Congo (AP) — Panic swept through eastern Congo’s second-largest city on Saturday as residents and soldiers fled by the thousands, scrambling to escape the looming advance of Rwanda-backed rebels.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN KABUMBA, SAM METZ and RODNEY MUHUMUZA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Doctors order 'absolute rest' for Pope Francis while he's being treated for a respiratory infection
item.description: ROME (AP) — Doctors on Saturday prescribed “absolute rest” for Pope Francis and modified the treatment for his respiratory tract infection, the Vatican said, a day after the 88-year-old pope was admitted to a hospital following a weeklong bout of bronchitis.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Netanyahu will reportedly hold a security meeting at Trump's deadline
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu is expected to hold a security meeting at 12 p.m. eastern, President Trump's deadline for Hamas to release the remaining hostages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel releases Palestinian prisoners linked to suicide bombings, attempted murders
item.description: Palestinian prisoners linked to suicide bombings and attempted murders against Israelis are among the 369 released Saturday by Israel in exchange for hostages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: American-Israeli hostage released by Hamas embraces his wife, family after being apart for nearly 500 days
item.description: American-Israeli hostage Sagui Dekel-Chen embraced his wife on Saturday, following his release from Hamas, after being apart from her for nearly 500 days.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Shattering the 'iron' ceiling: New series 'Guerrera' explores vital role of US female troops in combat
item.description: A new series released on Amazon, YouTube nd Google TV uncovers the little-known details about the team of women who tasked to special forces during the War on Terror and helped change the course of U.S. history.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kentucky bourbon bottles signed by the pope raise thousands for charity
item.description: It sounds like the opening line of a joke: What happens when the pope gets his hands on two bottles of Kentucky bourbon?
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER and DYLAN LOVAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Zelenskyy to Vance: Ukraine wants 'security guarantees' as Trump seeks to end Ukraine-Russia war
item.description: MUNICH (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Friday that his country wants “security guarantees” before any talks with Russia, as the Trump administration presses both countries to find a quick endgame to the three-year war.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, EMMA BURROWS and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: A drone pierced the outer shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant. Radiation levels are normal
item.description: CHERNOBYL NUCLEAR POWER STATION, Ukraine (AP) — A drone armed with a warhead hit the protective outer shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant early Friday, punching a hole in the structure and briefly starting a fire, in an attack Kyiv blamed on Russia. The Kremlin denied it was responsible.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV and EFREM LUKATSKY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Middle East latest: Palestinian militants name 3 Israeli men to be freed in next hostage release
item.description: The families of Israeli hostages still held by the Hamas militant group on Friday welcomed the “joyous news” that three men, all taken from the same kibbutz on Oct. 7, 2023, are expected to be freed on Saturday in exchange for more than 300 Palestinian prisoners held in Israeli jails.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Taiwan pledges to communicate and invest more in the US after Trump tariff threats
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — Taiwan President Lai Ching-te said Friday he would communicate more with the U.S. over President Donald Trump’s concerns over the chip industry and invest more in the U.S, hours after Trump ordered reciprocal tariffs on trade partners.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Steel tariffs could disrupt international bridge expansion
item.description: The steel 2-lane Starr-Camargo International Bridge in Rio Grande City, Texas, is gearing up for an expansion of lanes, however they might not be steel after 25% tariffs were imposed by the Trump administration, the bridge's owner Sam Vale told Border Report on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, WGN 9

item.title: Trump's taunting of Canada will pay dividends: Bill O'Reilly
item.description: “Those of use who live in the real world — not the ideological, crazy world — we know it’s just hyperbole.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation
